Farn Polrrrcai- PRACTTcEs LonrvrssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
February 23,2006
Thomas G. Schmidt
8713 Raven Hill Way
Antelope, CA 95843
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-06-009
Dear Mr. Schmidt:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the revolving door
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r This letter is solely based on the
facts presented to us in your request. The Commission does not act as a finder of fact
when issuing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) Our advice is applicable
only to the extent that the facts provided to us are correct, and all of the material facts
have been provided.2
QUESTION
Would the post-governmental employment provisions of the Act prohibit you
from communicating with or appearing before the Department of Conservation, Division
of Recycling, in connection with obtaining certification to operate a beverage container
recycling business?
CONCLUSION
Neither the "permanent ban" nor the "one-year ban" provisions would bar you
from representing your own business interest in the proceedings you describe before your
former agency employer.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
' We encou.age you to consider whether other laws such as Public Contract Code section 1 041 1 ,
may also be implicated by your situation. You may wish to consult with the Office of the Attorney General
regarding these other laws.
o+@+l


File No. A-06-009
Page No. 2
FACTS
The Department of Conservation administers the California Beverage Container
Recycling and Litter Reduction Act through the Division of Recycling. The Division of
Recycling certifies and regulates the operating activities of recycling -enters in
Califomia.
After your employrnent with the Department of Conservation, you wish to apply
for certification for one or more recycling centers. You have informed us that the
planned recycling centers will be operated as a sole proprietorship and will be wholly
owned and controlled by you. Any appearances or corlmunications with the Department
of Conservation would solely represent your personal business interests in certifying
recycling centers.
You currently work for the Department of Conservation as a Recycling Specialist
lI, a tank-and-fi1e, non-supervisory position. Your primary job responsibilitie-s include
inspecting recyclers and processors, leading special projects such as program
requirements and policy recolnmendations, conducting educational visits/training,
investigating complaints, and identifiring abandoned or uncertified recycling sites. The
Department of Conservation considers your position a designated empioyee with a
category 5 disclosure requirement.
ANALYSIS
Public officials who leave state service are subject to two types ofpost-
Soverlmental employment provisions under the Act,3 colloquially knownis the
"revolving door" prohibitions.o We discuss your question ,rrd", Lach of these provisions.
o Permanent Ban: The first restriction is the "permanent ban" prohibiting a former
state employee from "switching sides" and particip ating, for compensattn, in any
specific proceeding involving the State of California or assisting others in the
proceeding if the proceeding is one in which the former state employee
3 In addition, section 87407 prohibits certain state and local officials from making, participating in
making, or using their official position to influence decisions affecting persons with whori-tirey are
negotiating employment, or has any arrangement concerning employmint. (Section 874}l;reglrlation
18747.) However, since you solely present a question regarding your ability to start a businesi as a sole
proprietorship after leaving state employment, we need not advise regarding this provision.
a A comprehensive discussi,on of the elements of the two laris is set forth in the attached ,,Leaving
Your State Job? Post-Employment Restrictions May Affect You." We have attached this document for
your information. we do not repeat this information in the body of this letter.


File No. ,{-06-009
Page No. 3
participated while employed by the state (see sections 87401-87402, regtlation
ts74t.D.5
The permanent ban is a lifetime ban and applies to any judicial, quasi-judicial, or
other proceeding in which you participated while you served as a state administrative
official. "'Judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding' means any proceeding,
application, request for a ruling or other determination, contract, claim, controversy,
investigation, charge, accusation, arrest or other particular matter involving a specific
party or parties in any court or state administrative agency . . . ." (Section 87a00(c).)
Additionally, an official is considered to have "participated" in a proceeding if he or she
took part in the proceeding "personally, and substantially through decision, approval,
disapproval, formal written recommendation, rendering advice on a substantial basis,
investigation, or use of confidential information...." (Section 87400(d).)
Applying for certification of a recycling center is a particular matter involving a
specific party or parties before a state administrative agency and involves a request for a
determination with respect to the proposed recycling center. Thus, when you request
certification for a recycling center, it will comprise a judicial, quasi-judicial, or other
proceeding to which the permanent ban maypotentially apply.
However, the permanent ban applies only to proceedings you participated in
while an employee of the Department of Conservation. Based on the facts you have
provided your application for certification appears to be a "new proceeding.,, A new
proceeding typically involves different parties, different subject matter, or different
factual or legal issues from those considered in previous proceedings.
Under the facts presented, we conclude that your application for certification of
recycling centers would be considered a new proceeding so long as any associated
contacts with the Department of Conservation are solely related to your attempts for
certification. Accordingly, you would not be prohibited from applying for certification of
recycling centers un{er the permanent ban provisions of the Act after you leave your state
government position.
one-Year Ban: The second reskiction is the "one-year ban" prohibiting a state
employee from communicating, for compensation, with his or her former agency
for the purpose of influencing certain administrative or legislative action or
influencing certain proceedings. (See section 87406; regulation 1s746.1.)
o
5 Note that the permanent ban applies if you are compensated to act as a representative or agent of
another person. Under the Act, business entities are generally treated as separate persons, apart from the
orvners. Thus, the permanent ban may apply with respect to appearances on behalf of your business, unless
an exception applies. (Caplan Advice Letter, No. A-95-309.)


File No. 4-06-009
Page No. 4
The one-yearbanapplies for 12 months from the date of your separation from
employment with the Department of Conservation. While in effect, thetne-year ban
would prohibit any formal or informal appearance or oral or written communication with
your former agency for the principal purpose of supporting, promoting, influencing,
modiffing, opposing, delaying, or advancing the action or proceeding.
An appearance or communication includes, but is not limited to, conversing by
telephone or in person, coffesponding in writing or by electronic transrnission, attending
a meeting, and delivering or sending any communication. In contrast to the permanent
ban, which only applies to 'Judicial or quasi-judicial" proceedings, the one-year ban
applies to "any appearance or communication made for the purpose of influencing
administrative or legislative action,6 or influencing any action or proceeding involving
the issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permit, license, gr^t,or contract,
or the sale or purchase of goods or property.,,
The one-yearbanprovision applies only when a former anployee or official is
being compensated for his or her appearances or communications before his or her
former agerrcy onbehalf of anotherperson. (Section 87406;regulation 18746.1(b)(3).)
In reference to the one-year ban provision, regulation 18746J(bX4) creates an
exception to the general rule that business entities are treated as separate persons, apart
from the owners and provides that "[a]n appearance or communication mude by a public
official solely to represent his or her personal interests, as defined in [section
18702.4(b)(l)1, is not prohibited or limited by this section.,,
Section 18702.4b)(1) provides that an official must appear "in the same manner
as any other member of the general public before an agency in the course of its prescribed
govemmental function solely to represent himself or herself on a matter which is related
to his or her personal interests." Personal interest is defined to include:
(A) An interest in real property which is wholly owned by the official
or members of his or her immediate family.
(B) A business entity wholly owned by the official or members of his
or her immediate family.
(c) A business entity over which the official exercises sole direction
and control, or over which the official and his or her spouse jointly
exercise sole direction and control.
6 Section 82037 defines "legislative action" as "the drafting, introduction, consideration,
modification, enactment or defeat of any bill, resolution, amendment, report, nomination or other matter by
the Legislature or by either house or any committee, subcommittee, joint or select committee thereof, or by
a member or employee of the Legislature acting in his official capacity. 'Legislative action' also means the
action of the Governor in approving or vetoing any bill.,,


File No. A-06-009
Page No. 5
Under the facts you have submitted, when you apply to the Department of
Conservation for certification of recycling centers you will be representing your personal
business interest. The recycling centers will be wholly owned and operatedsolely by
you. When applyrng for certification, you will neither be representing another person nor
receiving compensation from another person as defined under the onelyear ban
provisions. For these reasons, we conclude that the one-year ban does not prohibit you
from applying for certification ofrecycling centers after you leave your state government
position.
If you have other questions on this matter,please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure

regarding these other laws.

QUESTION: Would the post-governmental employment provisions of the Act prohibit you
from communicating with or appearing before the Department of Conservation, Division
of Recycling, in connection with obtaining certification to operate a beverage container
recycling business?

CONCLUSION: Neither the "permanent ban" nor the "one-year ban" provisions would bar you
from representing your own business interest in the proceedings you describe before your
former agency employer.
' We encou.age you to consider whether other laws such as Public Contract Code section 1 041 1 ,
may also be implicated by your situation. You may wish to consult with the Office of the Attorney General
regarding these other laws.
